source_citation: Our World in Data — OurWorldinData.org/life-expectancy | CC BY. Data sources cited on figure: Riley (2005); Zijdeman et al. (2015); HMD (2025); UN WPP (2024); Bolt and van Zanden — Maddison Project Database 2023.
chart_title: Life expectancy vs. GDP per capita, 2022
chart_subtitle: The period life expectancy at birth, in a given year. GDP per capita is adjusted for inflation and differences in living costs between countries.

x_axis.title: GDP per capita
x_axis.title_verbatim: GDP per capita (international-$ in 2011 prices; plotted on a logarithmic axis)

size_encoding.note: Marker radius (visual area) encodes country population; not extracted into data.csv. Two reference circles printed in legend (1.4B and 600M).

data_source_note_verbatim: Note: GDP per capita is expressed in international-$ at 2011 prices.
notes: Our World in Data scatter (4 variables): x=GDP, y=life expectancy, marker size=population, marker color=continent. ~164 countries in source; extractor isolates visually distinct colored circles by per-continent color thresholding + morphological opening (2x2) to suppress same-color country-label text. Country names labeled inline next to ~20 large-population markers.
